title: Outstanding Loan Growth in China Sets Another Record Low
section_path: Outstanding Loan Growth in China Sets Another Record Low
section_path_text: Outstanding Loan Growth in China Sets Another Record Low
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly China Report, the latest released data shows that outstanding loan growth in China has fallen to 6.1% which has marked yet another record low. Image reference: Chart 1.jpg | assets/2026-02-26_outstanding-loan-growth-in-china-sets-another-record-low_img_chart1_b2a0d964a0fb.jpg
As we have discussed often in our Weekly China Reports, with the housing market remaining inundated with a huge amount of excess supply already available, Chinese banks are continuing to have difficulty in actually having lendees to lend to in the construction market. Image reference: Chart 2.jpg | assets/2026-02-26_outstanding-loan-growth-in-china-sets-another-record-low_img_chart2_fb584beb1027.jpg
As we have also discussed often, manufacturing production (which is less capital intensive and requires less lending than construction) has fared better and continues to help support China's steel market and offset the weakness in demand/construction for new homes.
